mnemonics.title: AND GATE MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: AND = ALL MUST BE 1. THINK: 'A' IN AND STANDS FOR 'ALL' - ALL INPUTS MUST BE 1 FOR OUTPUT 1.

mnemonics.title: OR GATE MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: OR = ONE OR MORE. THINK: 'O' IN OR STANDS FOR 'OUTPUT 1' IF 'ONE' OR 'MORE' INPUTS ARE 1.

mnemonics.title: XOR GATE MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: XOR = EQUALS 1 FOR 'E'XACTLY 'O'NE 'R'EASON (ONE INPUT 1). THINK OF X AS CROSSING - INPUTS MUST BE DIFFERENT.

mnemonics.title: NAND UNIVERSAL AID
mnemonics.content: NAND = NOT AND = 'NOT ALL'. IF NOT ALL INPUTS ARE 1, OUTPUT IS 1.

mnemonics.title: NOR UNIVERSAL AID
mnemonics.content: NOR = NOT OR = 'NONE'. IF NONE (ZERO) INPUTS ARE 1, OUTPUT IS 1.

acronyms.usage: Common family of digital circuits using bipolar junction transistors
Low-power digital logic family using MOSFETs
Maximum inputs a gate can handle
Number of gate inputs a single output can drive

visualTips.visualization: PICTURE A SERIES CIRCUIT - ALL SWITCHES (INPUTS) MUST BE CLOSED FOR CURRENT (OUTPUT) TO FLOW
PICTURE A PARALLEL CIRCUIT - ANY SWITCH (INPUT) CLOSED WILL ALLOW CURRENT (OUTPUT) TO FLOW

visualTips.visualize: PICTURE A LIGHT SWITCH - FLIPS THE STATE: ON BECOMES OFF, OFF BECOMES ON

visualTips.visualization: PICTURE A TWO-WAY SWITCH FOR LIGHTS - OUTPUT IS ON ONLY WHEN SWITCHES ARE IN DIFFERENT POSITIONS

analogies.analogy: LIKE A CHAIN: ALL LINKS MUST BE CONNECTED FOR THE CHAIN TO BE STRONG. IF ANY LINK BREAKS, THE CHAIN FAILS.
LIKE A GROUP PROJECT: GET AN A IF ANY TEAMMATE DOES THE WORK. MORE HELP JUST MEANS SUCCESS.
LIKE A NEGATION IN CONVERSATION: 'NOT HAPPY' MEANS 'SAD' - OPPOSITE OF INPUT.
LIKE A LIGHT SWITCH WITH TWO LOCATIONS: FLIPPING EITHER SWITCH CHANGES THE LIGHT STATE. SAME POSITIONS = LIGHT OFF, DIFFERENT = LIGHT ON.
LIKE A 'NOT ALL' RULE: IN A CLASS, IF NOT EVERYONE PASSES, THERE IS A CELEBRATION (OUTPUT=1). ONLY WHEN EVERYONE PASSES (ALL=1) DO WE NOT CELEBRATE (OUTPUT=0).
